reasoning: Q6 is already 3.2x faster than DuckDB (6ms vs 19.3ms), but substantial headroom remains. With only 1 iteration left, we must be aggressive. Priority 1 alone (thread scaling 16→64 cores) offers 3-4x speedup with minimal risk. Combined with SIMD improvements (aligned loads, AVX-512, simplified multiplication) and I/O optimizations, we can target 1.5-2ms (4x improvement from current 6ms).
focus_areas: Scale to all 64 cores (currently only using 16)
Upgrade SIMD: aligned loads + AVX-512 + simplified int64 multiplication
Optimize I/O: sequential madvise for surviving blocks after zone map pruning
Fix false sharing with proper cache-line padding for thread results
strategy_notes: This is the final iteration. Be aggressive and implement ALL selected optimizations simultaneously. The thread scaling (Priority 1) is the lowest-risk, highest-impact change and should be implemented first in the code. SIMD improvements (Priorities 2, 4, 5) are complementary and can be done together. I/O optimizations (Priority 3) target cold-data scenarios on HDD. False sharing fix (Priority 8) is essential when scaling to 64 threads. Skip Priority 6 (predicate reordering) as it offers minimal benefit with current parallel SIMD evaluation. Target: 1.5-2ms execution time (4x improvement from 6ms).
notes: Q6 already beats DuckDB by 3.2x. Final iteration should push the limits. Combined expected improvement: 3-4x from thread scaling + 1.5-2x from SIMD upgrades + 1.5x from I/O optimization = potential 6-10x total, but memory bandwidth will cap realistic gains at ~4x. Conservative target: 6ms → 1.5-2ms.
